meta_title: For Freelancers: portfolio building to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to portfolio building and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Freelancers: Portfolio Building to Solve Debugging Stalls
content.seo_description: Break through debugging blocks by building a strategic portfolio. Learn how to leverage Antigravity and Claude Code to ship projects faster as a freelancer.
content.answer_block: When you hit a debugging wall, stop fighting the syntax and pivot to building modular portfolio pieces. By standardizing your workflow with Antigravity and Claude Code, you isolate logic into reusable components. This turns a frustrating bug-fix session into a high-value asset that showcases your technical expertise to potential clients.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a terminal full of red stack traces, the clock is ticking on a client deliverable, and the logic feels like a tangled knot of spaghetti code. The panic is real, but the problem isn't your talent—it's your process. When you can't debug, you aren't just stuck; you're losing billable hours and momentum.

Smart freelancers don't spend hours wrestling with single lines of code. They build systems that make debugging obsolete. By shifting your mindset from 'fixing errors' to 'architecting portfolio-grade modules,' you transform every struggle into a polished case study. Let's use modern AI tooling to bypass the grind and ship professional-grade work on autopilot.

steps.title: Define the Portfolio Module
steps.description: Identify a recurring pain point in your client work and turn it into a standalone utility component. This reduces complexity and creates a reusable asset.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this code snippet for modularity. Extract the core logic into a clean, testable, and reusable function that I can add to my portfolio repository.

steps.title: Automate Error Isolation
steps.description: Use AI to wrap your problematic code in error boundaries to prevent application crashes and improve user experience.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Wrap this function in a robust error boundary that logs the stack trace to a JSON file and returns a graceful fallback UI state.

steps.title: Document for Clients
steps.description: Transform your code into a professional README that explains the 'why' behind the logic, making it portfolio-ready.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a technical documentation file for this module, written for a potential client. Include a 'Use Case' section and a 'Performance Metrics' table.

steps.title: Unit Test Generation
steps.description: High-quality portfolios require tests. Use AI to generate comprehensive test suites that prove your code works under pressure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Jest test suite for this module covering edge cases, null inputs, and performance bottlenecks.

steps.title: Ship to Showcase
steps.description: Push your refined, tested, and documented code to a public repository to demonstrate your technical rigor to future clients.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review my project structure for 'portfolio-readiness'. Suggest any final cleanups before I push this to GitHub.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /commit --message 'feat: add production-ready utility module for portfolio'

pro_tips: Never fix the bug in the main branch; always create a reproduction script first to verify the fix.
Treat your error logs as 'content'—a well-documented log file is a sign of a senior engineer.
Use Claude Code to standardize your file naming conventions across all projects for instant recognition.
Before finalizing a module, ask your AI to suggest a 'V2' enhancement to show future-thinking to clients.
Always include a 'Complexity Analysis' comment in your code to explain your architectural decisions.
content.next_steps: Once your first module is live, use the same process to build a 'Component Library' for your next client project, effectively doubling your development speed.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting bugs dictate your income and start building a portfolio that sells your services for you. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
